what_it_does: Documents the rule-core campaign rationale, verified existing substrates, scoped R1/R2/R3 work, exclusions, and loop mechanics for later composition by mobile and other surfaces.
observations: Frames the campaign around Tim's 2026-06-13 "B then A" direction: build the core piece first, then compose mobile and other surfaces from it.

Records existing substrates: generated --dna-* address scale, descriptive component records, split/reflow/proportion records, reading axes, identity-only dist constraints, serve/rebuild proof expectations, and type coverage enforcement.
Identifies gaps: chrome geometry still uses hardcoded px literals, component records describe controls without rendering them, and layout primitives need a scaffold that reads records live.
Scopes Round 1 into three pillars: geometry address migration plus proof, viewer-layout primitive, and typed refillable slot plus one component-render scaffold.
Sets boundaries that this is not the full mobile viewer or a full migration of all pieces, and records R1b changes around vocabulary structure, scale unification, and pending population/snap work.
Defines campaign process under docs/campaigns/rule-core with looped criterion-by-criterion work, proof runs, rc tags, and an eventual readiness report for a later mobile campaign.
standouts: Treats geometry, split layout, refillable slots, and record-rendered controls as reusable rule-core primitives rather than mobile-specific implementation details.
Makes the distinction between existing declarations and live rendering/proof explicit.
Records a concrete scale-structure finding: space vocabulary had lived in disconnected places and was partly unified for chrome-address generation.
conventions: Uses campaign rounds and labeled work packages such as R1, R2, R3, R1b, rc-3, and BACKLOG H3.
Uses emphatic uppercase terms to mark constraints, exclusions, and conceptual primitives, including NOT, EXISTS, WORK|PANEL, POPULATION, SNAP, and NAME-COLLISION.
Keeps evidence and scope notes inline rather than separating them into a formal table.
Uses arrows and parenthetical notes to connect findings to follow-up work packages.
concerns: The file mixes synthesis, scope, backlog, implementation findings, and process rules in one document, which makes the document dense.
Several terms are meaningful but only lightly defined inside this file, including "dist identity-only", "six proofs", "20/20 type coverage", "source ingest", and "canonical words".
The rc-3 update adds historical implementation detail into the boundary section, making Round 2 carry both exclusion and change-log roles.
notes: Briefing note: this file is the rule-core campaign synthesis that turns discovered design/code substrates into scoped primitives, proof expectations, and boundaries.
questions: What exact proof will serve as the spatial twin of lint-dna?
Which chrome surfaces are included in R1's "functioning chrome surfaces" scope?
What shape should the typed slot contract for {marks · face · compare} take?
Who resolves the surfaced spacing name collision, and where is that decision recorded?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the content: it acts as campaign ground-truth by recording rationale, verified substrate, planned pillars, exclusions, and loop process.
apparent_themes: Design vocabulary should be addressable, derived, and proof-covered.
Mobile viewer work should compose reusable primitives rather than invent local controls.
Identity preservation and explicit proof runs constrain campaign changes.
Campaign scope is separated from later full-surface migration.
intention_signals: It seems intended to align future rule-core work around a small set of reusable primitives, preserve evidence behind the campaign, and prevent the mobile viewer from absorbing foundational architecture work.
novelty: The file frames design-system work as a ledger of generated addresses, record-driven controls, layout dials, typed slots, proof obligations, and campaign rounds rather than as a conventional feature spec.
contribution: A campaign synthesis record that preserves rationale, verified substrate inventory, primitive definitions, scope boundaries, proof constraints, backlog signals, and process expectations for the rule-core work.
summary_for_embedding: A rule-core campaign synthesis describing why the core primitives come before the mobile viewer, what substrates already exist, what R1/R2/R3 will prove, what is out of scope, and how the campaign loop proceeds.
intention_for_embedding: The file was written to turn Tim's "B then A" direction and related evidence into actionable, bounded rule-core work that later surfaces can compose.
